name: DealCloud
blocker: API access is gated; a developer must be a user within an existing licensed DealCloud organization and have the required permissions assigned by an administrator.
one_liner: DealCloud is an AI-powered CRM and deal intelligence platform built for financial and professional services firms to manage relationships and pipeline execution.

evidence.claim: DealCloud is an AI-powered CRM and deal intelligence platform for financial and professional firms.
The DealCloud API provides REST endpoints for manipulating core site data, user management, and other functional areas.
API access requires specific user permissions enabled by an administrator and is not self-serve.
Search citation: Docs - DealCloud API Docs

api_notes: The API includes REST endpoints for Data (Rows/Cells), User Management, Schema, Backups, Publications, and Relationship Intelligence. It is intended for custom integrations and workflow automation.
auth_notes: Authentication involves using an API key/client secret to obtain an OAuth token (via /api/rest/v1/oauth/token). Bearer tokens are used in requests.
access_notes: Access requires an existing account with specific user group permissions ("API" capability) which are managed by an administrator. There is no public self-serve sign-up for the API.
mcp_notes: The documentation mentions an upcoming MCP server client preview starting in July 2026, but no functional server exists currently.
